{"id":"project-LICHEN-9smu","title":"loadng.c: access_counter can wrap around causing LRU inversion","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/routing/loadng.c
Lines: 31, 222, 251, 315

The access_counter is incremented on each cache access:

    static uint32_t access_counter;
    ...
    route_access_order[idx] = ++access_counter;

After ~4 billion accesses, access_counter wraps to 0. When this happens:
- New accesses get low values (near 0)
- Old entries from before wrap have high values (near UINT32_MAX)
- find_lru_slot_locked() line 194 compares: 'route_access_order[i] < oldest_access'
- This incorrectly identifies new entries as 'oldest' because 0 < UINT32_MAX

The result: recently-used routes get evicted while stale routes are retained.

Fix: Use signed comparison similar to timestamp handling, or periodically normalize access_order values.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:51:03.441145Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:51:03.441176Z"}
